ThreatDescription: Attacker injects malicious SQL code through user input fields
Mitigation: Use parameterized queries and input validation
SecurityRequirementDescription: All database queries must use parameterized statements to prevent SQL injection
TestCaseDescription: Verify that SQL injection attempts are blocked by parameterized queries

ThreatDescription: Malicious scripts injected into web pages viewed by other users
Mitigation: Sanitize inputs and encode outputs
SecurityRequirementDescription: All user inputs must be sanitized and outputs must be HTML encoded
TestCaseDescription: Verify that XSS payloads are properly escaped and do not execute

ThreatDescription: Attacker tricks users into performing unwanted actions
Mitigation: Implement CSRF tokens
SecurityRequirementDescription: All state-changing operations must validate CSRF tokens
TestCaseDescription: Verify CSRF tokens are generated and validated correctly

ComponentDescription: Handles user login and session management
ThreatDescription: Authentication mechanisms compromised allowing unauthorized access
Mitigation: Implement MFA and strong password policies
SecurityRequirementDescription: All user accounts must use MFA for enhanced security
TestCaseDescription: Verify MFA cannot be bypassed through various attack vectors

ComponentDescription: Handles user login and session management
ThreatDescription: Attacker steals or predicts session tokens to impersonate users
Mitigation: Generate secure random session tokens
SecurityRequirementDescription: Generate cryptographically secure random session tokens with HttpOnly and Secure flags
TestCaseDescription: Verify session tokens are secure and cannot be predicted or stolen

ComponentDescription: Handles user login and session management
ThreatDescription: Attackers use stolen credentials from other breaches
Mitigation: Implement rate limiting and account lockout
SecurityRequirementDescription: Implement rate limiting and account lockout after failed login attempts
TestCaseDescription: Verify rate limiting prevents brute force attacks

ThreatDescription: Users can access unauthorized resources or perform unauthorized actions
Mitigation: Implement RBAC with least privilege
SecurityRequirementDescription: Use RBAC to enforce least privilege principle for all API endpoints
TestCaseDescription: Verify users cannot access resources outside their authorization scope

ThreatDescription: Excessive API calls leading to service degradation
Mitigation: Implement API rate limiting and throttling
SecurityRequirementDescription: APIs must implement rate limiting to prevent abuse and DOS attacks
TestCaseDescription: Verify rate limiting is enforced on all API endpoints

ThreatDescription: APIs expose sensitive data in responses
Mitigation: Filter sensitive data from API responses
SecurityRequirementDescription: Ensure APIs do not expose sensitive data in responses unnecessarily
TestCaseDescription: Verify sensitive fields are filtered from API responses

ThreatDescription: Confidential data exposed due to inadequate encryption
Mitigation: Encrypt all sensitive data at rest
SecurityRequirementDescription: All sensitive data must be encrypted using AES-256 or equivalent
TestCaseDescription: Verify that sensitive data is properly encrypted in database

ThreatDescription: SQL injection through vulnerable stored procedures
Mitigation: Use parameterized stored procedures
SecurityRequirementDescription: All stored procedures must use parameterized queries
TestCaseDescription: Verify stored procedures are not vulnerable to SQL injection

ThreatDescription: Hard-coded or exposed database credentials
Mitigation: Use secrets management and environment variables
SecurityRequirementDescription: Database credentials must be stored in secure secrets management system
TestCaseDescription: Verify database credentials are not hard-coded or logged

ThreatDescription: Attacker injects malicious messages into queue
Mitigation: Validate and sanitize all queue messages
SecurityRequirementDescription: All messages must be validated before processing
TestCaseDescription: Verify malicious messages are rejected

ThreatDescription: Messages are modified in transit
Mitigation: Sign and encrypt messages
SecurityRequirementDescription: All messages must be cryptographically signed
TestCaseDescription: Verify message tampering is detected

ThreatDescription: Users upload malware or malicious files
Mitigation: Validate file types and scan for malware
SecurityRequirementDescription: Only allow whitelisted file types and scan all uploads
TestCaseDescription: Verify only allowed file types can be uploaded and malware is detected

ThreatDescription: Attacker accesses files outside intended directory
Mitigation: Sanitize file paths and use safe file operations
SecurityRequirementDescription: All file paths must be sanitized to prevent directory traversal
TestCaseDescription: Verify path traversal attacks are blocked

ThreatDescription: Attacker injects malicious content into logs
Mitigation: Sanitize log entries and use structured logging
SecurityRequirementDescription: All log entries must be sanitized to prevent log injection
TestCaseDescription: Verify log injection attempts are sanitized

ThreatDescription: Security events not properly logged for audit
Mitigation: Log all security-relevant events
SecurityRequirementDescription: All security-relevant events must be logged with sufficient detail
TestCaseDescription: Verify all security events are properly logged

ThreatDescription: Use of outdated or weak encryption protocols
Mitigation: Use TLS 1.3 or higher with strong cipher suites
SecurityRequirementDescription: All communications must use TLS 1.3 or higher with approved cipher suites
TestCaseDescription: Verify TLS 1.3 is enforced and weak ciphers are disabled

ThreatDescription: Attackers bypass certificate validation
Mitigation: Implement proper certificate validation
SecurityRequirementDescription: All TLS certificates must be properly validated including chain and revocation
TestCaseDescription: Verify certificate validation cannot be bypassed
